It dropped to the $95 mark last week during the Prime Day sale, but today’s deal takes an additional $15 off to make it easier on your wallet. items.title: Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time remake price leaks after pre-order listing goes live early

We know the game is real, we know it is scheduled to release this year, but we haven’t seen any gameplay or official confirmation on the price from Nintendo. But if a recent leak is indeed the real thing, retailers are already listing the game at a lower price than what I think most folks would expect.

items.title: Heybike offers up to $828 July 4th savings on e-bikes giving you the freedom to roam – all starting from $999
items.summary: Heybike has launched its 4th of July Sale event with up to $828 in savings, celebrating America’s Birthday. Among the bunch, we’re seeing the first post-launch inclusion of the brand’s newest Saturn Moped-Style e-bike with a FREE pair of mirrors tacked on ($69 value). You have the choice between the single-battery model at $999 shipped or the dual-battery model at $1,299 shipped. As we saw during its May launch that carried over into June, these two configurations are getting $100 markdowns from their $1,099 and $1,399 MSRPs, alongside the $69 FREE bundled mirrors for the best prices we have tracked. You can learn more about this all-new e-bike below, as well as browse the full sale lineup while it lasts through the upcoming holiday.

items.title: First deal strikes Samsung’s 2026 Odyssey G7 32-inch 4K dual-mode gaming monitor at $300 off
items.summary: Over at Samsung, you’ll find the latest Odyssey G7 (G73SH) 4K 165Hz gaming monitor listed for $849.99 shipped. This particular listing also includes a $50 upgrade credit that auto-applies during checkout, essentially dropping the monitor down to $799.99 shipped from its usual price of $1,100. This is the first deal we’re tracking for this model, and it takes $300 off its price tag to mark a new best price to beat. Amazon currently has the same model listed for $850, meaning you’re missing out on the $50 upgrade credit for additional savings. This is one of the first dual-mode gaming monitors from Samsung that can switch between 4K and FHD resolutions, and you can learn more about it below.
